summary: Generated scaffold endpoints (restaurants list/get, availability check) rewritten to delegate to the cross-network source clients so they hit real Surf-cleared SSR data instead of opentable.com placeholder paths that 404.
reason: The internal YAML spec defines /restaurants and /availability as REST paths with placeholder paths because OpenTable doesn't expose REST equivalents. The generated scaffold's client.Get(...) calls 404 against the live consumer site. Without these redirects the resource-style commands would always fail — and the user's headline workflow (find a restaurant, check availability) needs them to work. Each rewrite preserves the spec annotations (pp:endpoint, pp:method, pp:path, mcp:read-only) so the typed MCP surface keeps working; only RunE is changed.
validated_outcome: Live dogfood went from 16/78 failures (placeholder 404s) to 0/64 failures after the redirects landed. `sync` returns an empty success summary instead of erroring with stream issues; `restaurants list/get` and `availability check` route through the same Surf-cleared path goat/earliest use.
upstream_issue: Multiple gaps surfaced as Printing Press retro candidates: (1) generator emits unused helpers in helpers.go; (2) dogfood reimplementation_check sibling-import regex misses multi-segment internal/source/* paths; (3) generator does not emit .printing-press-patches.json on fresh print despite library CI requiring it.
